decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective immediate action is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader material to enhance lateral heat distribution away from the chip, reducing the local hot spot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity maximizes lateral heat spreading capability within the allowed bounds, directly addressing the high severity temperature violation.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
Material property set to upper bound; further improvements will require geometric changes if this step is insufficient.
